meaning: adj.可察觉的,显而易见的,感觉得到的
examples: Her foreign accent was barely perceptible.
Pasternak gave him a barely perceptible smile.
The price increase has had no perceptible effect on sales.
We followed the faint but perceptible outline of a gravel path heading east.
If they are to make a perceptible dent in the reflex statism of orthodox economics, they will need it.
